title: Kattikkayam Falls
tagline: Kattikkayam Falls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Kattikkayam Falls is located in the Kottayam district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Kattikkayam Falls is located in the Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It can be reached from Purukkakanam by way of Erratupetta to Illikakkal Kallu via Moonnilavu. The lake under the waterfall is the main attraction at Kattikkayam. It is considered one of the most unsafe waterfalls in Kerala due to the gaps in the rock under the lake can cause danger.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Illickal Kallu
topPlaces.description: Illikkal Kallu is a monolith located on top of the Illickal Malaa in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. The distance from kottayam railway station to illikal kallu is 57km.

topPlaces.name: Arakkulam
topPlaces.description: Arakkulam is a village in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala.It is 19 kilometres (12 mi) from Thodupuzha which is the gateway to the high ranges of Kerala. Thodupuzha-Idukki road goes through this village.

topPlaces.name: Elappally
topPlaces.description: Elappally is a village in Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district in Kerala, India. It is located 26 km from Thodupuzha, 85 km from Kochi, and 70 km from Kottayam.

topPlaces.name: Moolamattom
topPlaces.description: Moolamattom is the headquarters of Arakulam Panchayath in Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district in Kerala, India. It lies 22 km from Thodupuzha, on the banks of the Thodupuzha River, a tributary of the Muvattupuzha river.

topPlaces.name: Kudayathoor
topPlaces.description: Kudayathoor is a village in Idukki district which is located along the Thodupuzha-Puliyanmala road in the Indian state of Kerala. Kudayathoor is known for its scenery mostly because of the towering presence of Western ghats on one side.

topPlaces.name: Thalanadu
topPlaces.description: Thalanadu is a Southern Indian village, situated in the eastern part of Kottayam district in Kerala, between Vagamon, Moonnilavu and Teekoy

topPlaces.name: Melukavu
topPlaces.description: Melukavu (Melukavumattom) is a village in the eastern part of Meenachil Taluk in Kottayam district of Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Moolamattom hydro power station
topPlaces.description: The Moolamattom hydro power station is the power house of the Kerala State Electricity Board's Idukki Hydroelectric Project, the largest hydroelectric project in Kerala, India, with a capacity to generate 2398 gigawatt-hours of electricity annually. Located on the banks of Muvattupuzha River at Moolamattam in Arakulam Gram Panchayat of Thodupuzha Taluk of Idukki district, it is one of the largest underground hydroelectric power stations in India.

items.note: Illikkal Kallu is a monolith located on top of the Illickal Malaa in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. The distance from kottayam railway station to illikal kallu is 57km.

Arakkulam is a village in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala.It is 19 kilometres (12 mi) from Thodupuzha which is the gateway to the high ranges of Kerala. Thodupuzha-Idukki road goes through this village.
Elappally is a village in Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district in Kerala, India. It is located 26 km from Thodupuzha, 85 km from Kochi, and 70 km from Kottayam.

Moolamattom is the headquarters of Arakulam Panchayath in Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district in Kerala, India. It lies 22 km from Thodupuzha, on the banks of the Thodupuzha River, a tributary of the Muvattupuzha river.

Kudayathoor is a village in Idukki district which is located along the Thodupuzha-Puliyanmala road in the Indian state of Kerala. Kudayathoor is known for its scenery mostly because of the towering presence of Western ghats on one side.
Thalanadu is a Southern Indian village, situated in the eastern part of Kottayam district in Kerala, between Vagamon, Moonnilavu and Teekoy
Melukavu (Melukavumattom) is a village in the eastern part of Meenachil Taluk in Kottayam district of Kerala state, India.

The Moolamattom hydro power station is the power house of the Kerala State Electricity Board's Idukki Hydroelectric Project, the largest hydroelectric project in Kerala, India, with a capacity to generate 2398 gigawatt-hours of electricity annually. Located on the banks of Muvattupuzha River at Moolamattam in Arakulam Gram Panchayat of Thodupuzha Taluk of Idukki district, it is one of the largest underground hydroelectric power stations in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kattikkayam Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kattikkayam Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kattikkayam Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (61 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kattikkayam Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kattikkayam Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kattikkayam Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Kattikkayam Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kattikkayam Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Illickal Kallu, Arakkulam, Elappally, Moolamattom, Kudayathoor.
